page: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128g-960s is a memory-optimized server instance belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an AMD EPYC-Turin processor running at 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 16 vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a shared CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core, and includes 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside moderate multi-core throughput. The instance achieves top-tier results in stress-ng single-core CPU performance, single-threaded gzip compression, and single-threaded bzip3 decompression, placing it in the top 10% of tested servers. However, its multi-core performance in stress-ng and bzip3 decompression falls into the average category, reflecting the shared CPU allocation model. Memory performance is robust, with a PassMark memory score in the top 25% and average memory bandwidth speeds of 88.87 GB/sec for large block reads and 45.5 GB/sec for large block writes.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model lowers the entry barrier for high-memory configurations, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require continuous, dedicated CPU execution. The primary tradeoff is potential resource contention on the shared vCPUs, which is visible in the average multi-core benchmark ratings compared to its top-tier single-core performance. This server is suitable for database operations, caching layers, memory-intensive web serving, and general-purpose workloads requiring a large memory footprint.
description: Vultr vx1-m-16c-128g-960s is a memory-optimized virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It is equipped with an x86_64 AMD EPYC-Turin processor featuring 16 shared vCPUs, 128.0 GB of RAM, and 960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for single-threaded compression and decompression benchmarks. However, its multi-core processing and memory bandwidth metrics fall within the average range due to the shared CPU allocation. This resource profile offers a cost-effective solution for memory-dense workloads, such as database hosting and caching layers, where high memory capacity is prioritized over dedicated, continuous multi-core compute resources.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring shared AMD EPYC processors and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive workloads.

960 GB of local NVMe SSD storage included
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
